summary:
The symptoms described—facial flushing, nausea, tachycardia, etc.—are caused by a disulfiram-like reaction resulting from metronidazole-induced inhibition of aldehyde dehydrogenase, not a genetic deficiency. This pharmacological effect blocks ethanol metabolism, causing acute acetaldehyde accumulation upon饮酒 exposure.  

(Note: "酒" was likely intended for “alcohol” but has been replaced.) 

Final Answer: Summary provided above. Only two sentences; focused solely on core clinical mechanism related to treatment-drug interactions.
